doom.driver: US-Iran talks begin in Switzerland as Tehran says it closed Strait of Hormuz. The US disputed Iran's claim the waterway is shut, a move Tehran says was a response to Israeli attacks in Lebanon.
doom.blurb: WORLD'S MOST IMPORTANT CHOKEPOINT: OPEN? CLOSED? YES.
doom.summary: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is shut. The U.S. says ships are sailing through just fine. Both cannot be right, which means either someone is bluffing or someone is about to find out they weren't. Meanwhile, JD Vance has flown to Switzerland, which is either a sign that these talks are serious or that Switzerland is having a very stressful weekend. Twenty percent of global oil transits the Strait daily — a fun fact that tends to focus the mind.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: Conflicting claims over Strait of Hormuz closure inject fresh uncertainty into already fragile US-Iran diplomacy, with Vance, Kushner, and Witkoff all wheels-down in Geneva.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: Colombia's presidential runoff pits a hardliner vowing full military confrontation with cartels against a leftist backing talks — either outcome reshapes a major narco-conflict zone.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: Booz Allen report warning that Chinese AI models may produce more vulnerable code for U.S. government users is concerning but still in the 'red flag' rather than 'red alert' tier.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Bolivia declares state of emergency with military deployed against protesters — Latin America adding a third country to its instability column this week.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.detail: Quiet day on the AI frontier — the most alarming AI story involves plagiarized dictionary definitions, which is bad but not extinction-adjacent.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing — weather-dominated scene, thick fog rolling off Lac Léman pressing against the stone facade of a grand Geneva lakeside building in pre-dawn darkness, camera positioned at mid-distance across frost-glazed cobblestones looking toward tall arched windows glowing amber from within, deep navy sky pressing down with near-zero visibility, bare winter trees dissolved into grey murk at the edges of the frame, three flagpoles rising from the entrance steps bearing the Stars and Stripes, Iranian green-white-red tricolor, and Pakistani crescent flag, all hanging limp and heavy with moisture in the still foggy air, a row of black Mercedes S-Class vehicles idling at the kerb with exhaust vapour rising and dissolving into the fog, their headlights cutting weak orange cones into the murk, and far beyond the building's silhouette barely visible through the haze a massive dark hull of a crude oil tanker sits motionless on the flat grey-green water of the strait rendered as a ghostly smudge on the horizon, smoldering orange light bleeding upward from somewhere behind the Alpine ridge as if distant fires are the only warmth in a frozen world, deep navy and threatening amber dominating every shadow.

dawn.driver: Yale study finds nearly half of older adults improved with age. A long-term Yale study is challenging one of the biggest myths about aging. Nearly half of adults over 65 improved physically, mentally, or both over time, despite the common belief that aging means decline.
dawn.blurb: AGING IS NOT WHAT WE THOUGHT
dawn.summary: On a day dominated by war talks, geopolitical friction, and cascading crises, Yale researchers quietly detonated one of medicine's most stubborn myths: that growing old means inevitable decline. Nearly half of adults over 65 actually improved — physically, mentally, or both — over the study period. That's not a rounding error; that's a fundamental rewrite of how we understand human resilience across a lifetime. In a feed full of doom, this finding lands differently: the bodies and minds carrying us through hard times are more capable of renewal than we ever assumed.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: A long-term Yale study found nearly half of adults over 65 improved physically or mentally over time, overturning decades of assumptions about aging as inevitable decline.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: 20,000 runners united across South Africa's deep racial divides at the world's largest ultramarathon, with one reporter noting inequality seemed to 'melt away' at the starting line.

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: US and Iranian delegations arrived in Switzerland to begin direct peace talks, with technical-level negotiations representing the most substantive diplomatic contact in years.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: Scientists discovered that thawing permafrost triggers increased rock weathering that absorbs CO2, with some regions fully offsetting their own carbon emissions through this hidden geological process.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Research published in Cell found that slow breathing measurably modulates brain function and risk behavior, offering a simple, free tool for mental resilience that anyone can use today.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Extreme close-up filling the entire frame, a single aged paper graph scroll pressed flat against a warm amber-lit wooden surface, the camera pressed so close that individual trajectory lines dominate everything. Deep golden hour amber light rakes across the paper from the left, casting long warm shadows that make the texture of the aged document glow like honey. The central visual fact: while one thick faded line slopes gently downward across the scroll, four or five individual trajectory lines branch and curve unmistakably upward like saplings bending toward light, diverging from the average in defiance of gravity. The paper itself is warm ivory yellowed at the edges, slightly curled, suggesting years of handling across a long study. At the extreme edges of the frame, out of focus in soft bokeh, the warm teal shadow of an open window and the blurred amber glow of afternoon sun bleed into the corners. The upward-branching lines are rendered in warm copper and soft teal pixel strokes, precise and deliberate. The downward average line is thin and gray, visually subordinate, almost apologetic beside the rising individual paths. The emotional register is quiet revelation — the paper itself seems to be the source of the warm light, as if the data is illuminating the room.
